skill.name: peer-review
skill.description: Structured scientific peer review toolkit for evaluating manuscripts, grants, presentations, methodology, statistics, reproducibility, ethics, figures, and reporting standards.
skill.summary: Guide rigorous scientific peer review with structured checks and discipline-specific references.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but the weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from scientific review terminology and reference material. The only confirmed risk is legitimate external command guidance: the skill allows Bash and includes local Python command examples for schematic generation and slide conversion.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash support and gives Python command examples for generating schematics and converting presentation PDFs to images. This appears legitimate for the workflow, but marketplace users should know the skill can guide command execution against local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and Bash permission are explicit. I found no shell metacharacter injection pattern or secret access, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Not Active Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The detected hardcoded URLs are citations to public reporting standards, not fetch calls or exfiltration endpoints. They are informational references used to support scientific manuscript review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL lines are labeled as references in markdown. No evidence found of code that sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Matches Are Scientific Review Terms
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts map to manuscript-review concepts such as bias, statistical power, reporting standards, and data availability. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, system probing, or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown guidance for scientific assessment. I found no executable scanner, cryptographic API, or reconnaissance workflow in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Enabled Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill metadata permits Bash, and the instructions include local Python commands. This is not malicious, but it increases execution risk if a user runs commands without checking paths and inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Bash support and command examples are present in the skill. The commands appear fixed and local, so this is a warning-level pattern.

content.value_statement: Peer review can miss methodological, statistical, and reporting problems. This skill guides structured manuscript, grant, and presentation review with checklists and references.
actual_capabilities: Guides section-by-section review of abstracts, methods, results, discussion, references, and figures.
Checks statistical rigor, study design, sample size, controls, bias, and reproducibility.
Maps manuscripts to reporting standards such as CONSORT, PRISMA, STROBE, ARRIVE, MIAME, and MINSEQE.
Structures review reports into summary statements, major comments, minor comments, and author questions.
Supports presentation review by recommending image-based slide inspection and slide-specific feedback.
Provides common issue references for methodological, interpretation, visualization, and reporting problems.
limitations: Does not replace expert judgment from a qualified reviewer in the manuscript discipline.
Cannot verify unpublished raw data, hidden analysis code, or journal records unless provided.
May require user-supplied manuscript text, figures, slides, or proposal sections for detailed review.
Includes Bash-based workflow examples, so users should inspect commands before running them.

use_cases.title: Journal Manuscript Review
use_cases.description: Evaluate a submitted manuscript for methodological soundness, statistical reporting, reproducibility, and clear conclusions.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Assessment
use_cases.description: Review research aims, feasibility, experimental design, controls, risks, and expected impact before panel discussion.

use_cases.title: Scientific Presentation Feedback
use_cases.description: Inspect slide clarity, visual design, data presentation, citations, and scientific flow with slide-specific comments.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Manuscript Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a draft manuscript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this manuscript draft. Provide a short summary, three major concerns, three minor concerns, and a publication recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Methods and Statistics Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the methods and results sections for study design, controls, sample size, statistical tests, effect sizes, and reproducibility gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Reporting Standards Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Identify the appropriate reporting guideline for this study type. Check missing CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, ARRIVE, or related checklist items.

prompt_templates.title: Full Peer Review Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for journal or grant submission.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a complete peer review report with summary, strengths, major comments, minor comments, ethical concerns, reproducibility issues, and questions for authors.

output_examples.input: A reviewer provides a clinical trial manuscript and asks for a structured critique.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes the study, checks trial design and CONSORT elements, flags missing allocation details, and separates major and minor comments.

output_examples.input: A researcher asks whether a methods section supports reproducibility.
output_examples.output: The response identifies missing software versions, unclear sample processing, absent power analysis, and specific additions needed for replication.

output_examples.input: An advisor asks for feedback on a scientific slide deck.
output_examples.output: The response reviews slide readability, figure clarity, contrast, citations, narrative flow, and lists issues by slide number.

best_practices: Provide manuscript type, target journal or funder, and discipline before requesting review.
Ask for major validity issues first, then request minor clarity and formatting comments.
Include methods, results, figures, and supplementary details when reproducibility matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to generate hostile or personal reviewer comments.
Do not ask for a publication decision without providing enough methodological context.
Do not run suggested Bash commands on sensitive files without reviewing paths and outputs.

faq.question: Can this skill review grant proposals?
faq.answer: Yes. It can evaluate aims, methodology, feasibility, rigor, reproducibility, and impact for research applications.

faq.question: Does it support multiple scientific fields?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses general review principles and references reporting standards across clinical, biological, animal, omics, and related fields.

faq.question: Can it check statistical methods?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts checks for assumptions, sample size, multiple testing, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and missing data handling.

faq.question: Can it verify reporting guideline compliance?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps identify relevant guidelines and missing checklist elements, but final compliance depends on the supplied manuscript details.

faq.question: Does it analyze figures and slides?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for figure integrity, data visualization, accessibility, and image-based presentation review.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes Bash support and local Python command examples. No malicious network or exfiltration behavior was found.
